summary: permcserver ships a VLESS/WebSocket TCP-relay backdoor as a fake index.html and self-deletes on require()
details: On require(), index.js spawns a worker that loads index.html via koffi and invokes the exported vserver_start() function. The file named index.html is not HTML but a Linux x86_64 ELF shared library whose symbols (vless_ws.c, ws_write_frame, dial_tcp, relay_thread) and hardcoded UUID 4916e0f5-8abc-4ad4-ba5d-158582c60d85 implement a VLESS-over-WebSocket proxy: any remote peer presenting the UUID over a WebSocket upgrade can instruct the host to open TCP connections to arbitrary destinations and relay traffic through it. After the worker reports the relay has started, index.js recursively removes its own package directory (fs.rmSync(__dirname, {recursive:true, force:true})), erasing the loader and ELF from disk while the proxy continues running from the library already mapped into the worker. The native payload disguised with an HTML extension and the post-start self-deletion are not consistent with any legitimate function.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
